title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE PILI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ SAN JOSE PILI ANNEX IN BARANGAY SAN JOSE, MUNICIPALITY OF PILI, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR FROM THE PILI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS SAN JOSE PILI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 1450; 109 OG No. 7, 818 (February 18, 2013)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10286, November 14, 2012 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE PILI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL ГўВЂВ“ SAN JOSE PILI ANNEX IN BARANGAY SAN JOSE, MUNICIPALITY OF PILI, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR FROM THE PILI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS SAN JOSE PILI NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School. ГўВЂВ“

The Pili National High School ГўВЂВ“ San Jose Pili Annex in Barangay San Jose, Municipality of Pili, Province of Camarines Sur is hereby separated from the Pili National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as San Jose Pili National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

ГўВЂВ“ All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Pili National High School ГўВЂВ“ San Jose Pili Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the San Jose Pili National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

ГўВЂВ“ The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the DepartmentГўВЂВ™s program the operationalization of the San Jose Pili National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current yearГўВЂВ™s appropriations of the Pili National High School ГўВЂВ“ San Jose Pili Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation.

ГўВЂВ“ The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

ГўВЂВ“ This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.)

JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 3, 2011 and July 24, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.)

EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.)

MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

NOV 14 2012

(Sgd.)

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
